extract.name: Shift U: AI Workforce Programs in Edmonton
extract.summary: A hub page for Shift U, an Edmonton-based AI workforce training company. It presents three tiered programs that train teams to adopt AI workflows: a cohort-based AI Operator Certification, on-site Private Team Training, and a 12-week Workforce Transformation engagement. Each program lists deliverables, a duration, and an outcome guarantee.
extract.pricing: AI Operator Certification: $7,500 per seat. Private Team Training: flat program fee $22,500. Workforce Transformation: flat engagement $55,000.
included: AI Operator Certification — Certified Shift U AI Operator credential
AI Operator Certification — Working prompt library tuned to your business
AI Operator Certification — 90 day internal rollout plan with named owners
AI Operator Certification — Live coaching access for the full cohort window
AI Operator Certification — 100 percent pass guarantee on exam
Private Team Training — Team-wide adoption of 3 core AI workflows
Private Team Training — Custom SOPs documented for sales, ops and admin
Private Team Training — Manager dashboard tracking time saved per role
Private Team Training — Two follow-up sessions at day 30 and day 60
Private Team Training — Zero Bottleneck Promise: a working AI workflow live in 30 days, or we keep working at no charge
Workforce Transformation — Audit and roadmap signed off by your leadership team
Workforce Transformation — AI-rebuilt workflows in 5 core functions
Workforce Transformation — Executive coaching for the leadership team
Workforce Transformation — Quarterly business review for the first 12 months
Workforce Transformation — Transformation Guarantee: measurable revenue or margin lift inside 90 days
keyPoints: Three-step delivery rhythm. Step 1: Discovery — 30 minute discovery call. Step 2: Cohort or on-site delivery — real workflows, real revenue context, real time. Step 3: Rollout and measurement — weekly review, time saved per role, revenue impact, monthly reporting.
AI Operator Certification: 6 weeks, cohort based.
Private Team Training: 4 weeks, on-site and remote.
Positioning: the businesses pulling ahead are not the ones with the biggest AI budget, but the ones whose team actually uses AI daily.
proof: Quote from Joshua, Sales and Discovery Lead at Shift U: 'The Alberta operators who are pulling ahead right now are not the ones with the biggest AI budget. They are the ones whose team actually uses it daily.'
faq: How do you decide which program is right for my business?
What if my team does not adopt what we learn?
Can we customize the curriculum to our industry?
Do you support businesses outside Edmonton?
extract.capgRemoved: Removed all Canada-Alberta Job Grant (CAJG) funding content. Specifically stripped: (1) the tagline suffix "funded through 2028"; (2) all "After Canada-Alberta Job Grant" net prices and grant-covered amounts — AI Operator Cert "$2,550 net (grant covers $4,950)", Private Team Training "$7,650 net (grant covers $14,850)", Workforce Transformation "$18,700 net (grant covers $36,300)" — kept only the real sticker prices ($7,500 / $22,500 / $55,000); (3) the how-it-works step originally "Discovery and CAJG paperwork" with "Canada-Alberta Job Grant submission" — reduced to plain discovery; (4) the entire grant FAQ "Is the Canada-Alberta Job Grant actually that high? Yes. CAJG covers up to 66 percent of eligible training costs, up to $10,000 per trainee per year, with funding committed through 2028. Adam handles the submission paperwork for every program we run."; (5) badge/stat blocks "66% covered by the Canada-Alberta Job Grant", "$10,000 per trainee, per year", "Funded through 2028", and "Based on the 66 percent Canada-Alberta Job Grant ceiling... Adam handles the paperwork."; (6) target-audience framing "Alberta SMBs claiming CAJG / You are an Alberta employer with between 5 and 50 staff. The Canada-Alberta Job Grant covers up to 66 percent of training, up to $10,000 per trainee, funded through 2028." The proof quote from Joshua was kept but is the only place "Alberta operators" remains as it is a genuine testimonial, not grant framing.
